text.title: Hook Bank Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when running a hook bank. Each pairs symptom + root cause + fix keyed to the methodology rules.

antipattern.symptom: Bank rows tagged SPIKED or FLOPPED without the impression ratio matching the threshold; weekly review shows recency-biased classifications.
antipattern.root-cause: Author overweights the most recent post or the one with social proof; ignores the 90-day median.
antipattern.fix: Move bucket assignment into the validator script: bucket derived from impressions_24h / platform_medians[platform]. Reject manual overrides. Rule r2.

antipattern.symptom: patterns_active grows to 25+ entries; classification becomes meaningless; remix-step takes longer than blank-page writing did.
antipattern.root-cause: Every SPIKED post triggers a new pattern; no pruning discipline.
antipattern.fix: Cap at 15 (r6). Monthly review must demote one when promoting a new one. Validator rejects bank files with patterns_active.length > 15.

antipattern.symptom: Bank file last committed 8 weeks ago; new posts written from scratch despite the bank's existence.
antipattern.root-cause: No weekly cadence; reviewed_at field absent or stale.
antipattern.fix: Calendar weekly review (15 min). Pre-commit hook fails if reviewed_at > 14 days old when bank file changes. Surface staleness in the build-update post.

antipattern.symptom: Author opens the post editor without re-reading the SPIKED top-10; draft time creeps back to 20 minutes.
antipattern.root-cause: No trigger linking "open editor" to "read SPIKED top-10".
antipattern.fix: Stash the SPIKED top-10 view as the editor's starting screen (browser bookmark, terminal alias, sheet pinned tab). Rule r4 becomes a workflow not a wish.
